word_list.pronunciation: fɹˈaɪʔn̩ skˈɛɹ tˈɛɹɪfˌaɪ ˌɛsbˈiː ˌaʊɾəv wˈʌnz wˈɪts

senses.definition: to do something that makes someone really scared

examples.x: The haunted house was so realistically designed that it frightened me out of my wits.
Walking through the dark alley at night, the sound of footsteps behind me terrified me out of my wits.

senses.definition: a state in which one sweats because of being really scared or anxious

examples.x: I break into a cold sweat whenever I have to speak in front of a large audience.
She often breaks out in a cold sweat during exams.
The thought of heights makes him break into a cold sweat.
He broke into a cold sweat when he realized he had lost his passport.
I broke out in a cold sweat after narrowly avoiding a car accident.
She broke into a cold sweat when she heard the news of her friend's accident.
I will break into a cold sweat if I have to confront my fear of spiders.
The horror movie left me in a cold sweat.
The athlete finished the race, drenched in a cold sweat.
She woke up from a nightmare, covered in a cold sweat.

word_list.pronunciation: tʃˈɪl ˌɛsbˈiː tə ðə bˈoʊn mˈæɹoʊ

senses.definition: to make someone become extremely terrified

examples.x: The haunting melody of the song chills us to the bone.
The ghostly apparition in the old house chilled him to the marrow.
The thought of facing my worst fear tomorrow will chill me to the bone.

word_list.pronunciation: tʃˈɪl ɔːɹ ʃˈɪvɚ ɹˈʌn ˌʌp ɔːɹ dˌaʊn wˈʌnz spˈaɪn

senses.definition: used when one does or says something that makes someone become extremely scared or excited

examples.x: The suspenseful movie always sends chills running up my spine.
The haunting melody in the song sends chills running up her spine.
The upcoming haunted attraction will send chills running down my spine.
The thought of visiting the haunted forest next week will send chills running up their spines.
The haunting melody of the music box sends shivers running up my spine.

senses.definition: to cause extreme worry, distress, or discomfort for others

examples.x: Her incessant nagging is the death of me; I can't take it anymore.
That job was the death of him; the stress and long hours took a toll on his health.

senses.definition: in a state of great anticipation, often holding one's breath

examples.x: I wait with bated breath for the announcement of the winner.
The audience watches the thrilling performance with bated breath.
With bated breath, she listens for the sound of footsteps approaching.
We watched the final moments of the game with bated breath.
The entire room held their breath with bated breath as the results were revealed.
He read the last chapter of the book with bated breath, anxious to know the ending.

senses.definition: (of a person) to involuntarily shake one's body due to feeling nervous, afraid, or cold

examples.x: He shakes like a leaf every time he has to speak in public.
She shook like a leaf when she encountered the wild bear in the woods.
Tomorrow, she will shake like a leaf as she gives her presentation to a large audience.
If I were to encounter a ghost, I would shake like a leaf.

word_list.pronunciation: skˈɛɹ ðə lˈɪvɪŋ ɔːɹ kɹˈæp ˌaʊɾəv ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: to extremely frighten someone

examples.x: The horror movie I'm watching right now is really scaring the crap out of me.
That haunted house we visited last Halloween scared the living crap out of us.
The horror movie they'll be watching later will scare the living crap out of everyone.

senses.definition: to do or say something that makes someone become extremely frightened or worried

examples.x: The horror movie I'm watching right now scares me witless.
That haunted house last Halloween scared us witless.
The thunderstorm last night scared me witless with its booming thunder.

senses.definition: to frighten or shock someone in order to improve or correct their behavior

examples.x: The documentary about prison life scares me straight, making me reconsider my choices.
The tough love approach they use in the rehabilitation program scares him straight, motivating him to turn his life around.

senses.definition: to do something that makes someone extremely scared

examples.x: The horror movie I'm watching right now scares me shitless.
That haunted house last Halloween scared us shitless.
The upcoming roller coaster ride will scare me shitless.

senses.definition: to become extremely scared or surprised, causing a strong physical reaction

examples.x: The sudden loud bang makes me jump out of my skin every time.
Whenever I see a spider, I jump out of my skin with fear.
The loud noise made her jump out of her skin.
I almost leaped out of my skin when I saw the spider on my desk.
The unexpected fireworks caused everyone to jump out of their skin.

word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk wˈʌnz hˈɛɹ stˈænd ˌɑːn ˈɛnd

senses.definition: to frighten or shock someone very suddenly or severely

examples.x: The horror movie I'm watching right now makes my hair stand on end.
The eerie sound of footsteps in the empty house makes my hair stand on end.
That haunted house we visited last Halloween made our hair stand on end.
The chilling revelation in the book I read yesterday made my hair stand on end.

word_list.pronunciation: ʃˈeɪk kwˈeɪk tɹˈɛmbəl ɪn wˈʌnz bˈuːts ʃˈuːz

senses.definition: to shake involuntarily as a response to extreme fear

examples.x: The idea of giving a presentation in front of a large audience makes me quake in my shoes.
The sight of the charging bull made him quake in his boots.
I know that meeting my significant other's parents for the first time will make me quake in my boots.
The eerie noises in the dark forest have me trembling in my boots.
When I heard the loud crash, I trembled in my boots with fear.
The haunted house we visited last Halloween had us all trembling in our shoes.

word_list.pronunciation: skˈɛɹ fɹˈeɪʔn̩ ðə lˈɪvɪŋ dˈeɪlaɪts ˌaʊɾəv ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: to shock or frighten someone very suddenly or severely

examples.x: The sudden loud noise from the fireworks scared the daylights out of me.
The haunted house we visited last Halloween scared the daylights out of everyone.
When the dog jumped out from behind the bushes, it scared the living daylights out of me.
The thought of skydiving next month is already scaring the daylights out of me.

senses.definition: in a state of great anxiety, suspense, or excitement while waiting for something

examples.x: I am on tenterhooks waiting to hear back about the job interview.
She is on tenterhooks as she awaits the results of the medical tests.
He was on tenterhooks until the final exam grades were announced.
We were on tenterhooks during the tense penalty shootout.
They will be on tenterhooks until the winner of the competition is announced.
I will be on tenterhooks until I receive a response to my scholarship application.
The suspenseful movie is keeping me on tenterhooks until the mystery is solved.
The ongoing negotiation process has everyone on tenterhooks.

senses.definition: in a very restless and agitated state, often due to being shocked or frightened

examples.x: The dog ran off like a scalded cat when it heard the loud thunder.
She always jumps up and runs away like a scalded cat whenever she sees a spider.
The startled rabbit darted away like a scalded cat when it heard a noise in the bushes.
He jumped back like a scalded cat when he accidentally touched the hot stove.

word_list.pronunciation: skˈɛɹ ðə lˈɪvɪŋ ɔːɹ ʃˈɪt ˌaʊɾəv ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: to intensely frighten or shock someone

examples.x: The horror movie I'm watching right now scares the shit out of me.
That haunted house we visited last Halloween scared the living shit out of us.
The thunderstorm last night scared the living shit out of me with its booming thunder.
The upcoming roller coaster ride will scare the shit out of me.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈuːɾᵻd tʊ ɔːɹ ɪnðə spˈɑːt ɔːɹ flˈoːɹ ɔːɹ ɡɹˈaʊnd

senses.definition: unable to move or act due to being extremely shocked, surprised, or frightened

examples.x: He was rooted to the ground when he saw the car accident unfold before his eyes.
We were rooted to the spot, frozen with fear, as the loud explosion echoed through the air.
Tomorrow, they will be rooted in the ground in awe as they witness the breathtaking natural phenomenon.
The shocking revelation leaves him rooted in the floor, unable to comprehend what he just heard.

senses.definition: to cause a strong feeling of fear in someone

examples.x: The sinister revelation in the movie makes my blood run cold every time I watch it.
The eerie silence and dark shadows in the abandoned house make my blood run cold as I cautiously explore.
The terrifying scream from the haunted house made my blood run cold as I hurriedly ran away.

word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk wˈʌn skˈɪn flˈɛʃ kɹˈɔːl kɹˈiːp

senses.definition: to experience a feeling of discomfort or disgust that causes a tingling feeling
with great effort to avoid injury or save one's life

examples.x: The hiker clings to the cliff's edge for dear life, trying to find a secure foothold.
The swimmer kicks and strokes for dear life as the powerful current threatens to pull them away.
She held onto the handrail for dear life as the roller coaster plunged down at high speed.
